title: rubus × retzii
intro: Rubus × retzii, also known as the hybrid raspberry or raspberry rose, is a cross between the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the fragrant raspberry (Rubus loganobaccus), resulting in a unique and versatile plant. The leaves and roots of Rubus × retzii have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, offering numerous health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, boosted immune function, and reduced risk of certain cancers. Rubus × retzii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in culinary applications, such as jams and preserves, to take advantage of its sweet and fragrant flavor.
